name: Chocolate Chunk Brownies
recipeIngredient: 9 ounces Swiss dark chocolate, cut into 1/4-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees F (165 C). Position a rack in the center of the oven.
2. Line a 13-by-9 inch baking pan with double thickness of aluminum foil, extending 2 inches beyond the two short ends. Fold the overhang down along the sides of the pan and butter the bottom of the foil-lined pan.
3. In a medium bowl, stir together flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
4. Place semisweet chocolate in a large bowl.
5. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar, butter, corn syrup, and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon until the butter melts, sugar dissolves, and mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat and pour hot syrup over chocolate. Let stand 1-2 minutes to melt chocolate. Whisk until smooth.
6. Add eggs one at a time, blending until smooth. Whisk in vanilla and flour mixture, mixing until batter is smooth. Fold in 1 cup walnuts and 6 ounces Swiss chocolate chunks.
7. Scrape batter into prepared pan and spread evenly with a spatula. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup walnuts and 3 ounces chocolate chunks over the top. Bake for 40-50 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted into the center comes out with few moist crumbs clinging to it.
8. Cool brownies in the pan on a wire rack. Cover pan with plastic wrap and let set at room temperature for at least 6 hours or overnight. Invert onto a large plate or cutting board. Remove foil carefully, invert again onto a smooth surface, and cut into 24 bars. Store at room temperature in an airtight container for up to five days.
